title: Spawn a thread
instruction:
`thread::spawn` runs a closure in a new OS thread.

`.join()` waits for it to finish.

Run this.

codeChecks.message: Use thread::spawn to create a thread.
Call join() to wait for the thread to complete.

successMessage: Output order varies — that is concurrency. join() ensures we wait for the spawned thread before main exits.

title: move closure in thread
instruction:
Threads need `move` closures to take ownership of captured data.

Run this.
successMessage: move transfers message into the thread closure. Without move, Rust cannot guarantee message outlives the thread.

title: Arc<T> — shared across threads
instruction:
`Arc<T>` (Atomic Reference Count) is `Rc<T>` for multiple threads — thread-safe.

Run this.

codeChecks.message: Wrap the shared data in Arc::new.
Clone the Arc before passing to each thread.

successMessage: Arc allows multiple threads to share ownership. The data is freed when the last Arc drops, regardless of thread order.

title: Mutex<T> — exclusive access
instruction:
`Mutex<T>` ensures only one thread accesses the data at a time.

Run this.
successMessage: Arc<Mutex<T>> is the standard pattern for shared mutable state across threads. lock() acquires the mutex, returning a MutexGuard that releases it when dropped.

title: Channels — message passing
instruction:
`std::sync::mpsc` provides a multi-producer, single-consumer channel.

Run this.
successMessage: tx sends, rx receives. rx is an iterator — when all senders drop, it ends. Message passing avoids shared state entirely.

title: Multiple producers
instruction:
Clone the transmitter for multiple senders.

Run this.
successMessage: mpsc = multi-producer single-consumer. Clone tx for each sender. Drop the original tx so the receiver knows when all senders are done.

title: Rayon-style data parallelism
instruction:
Use `std::thread::scope` (Rust 1.63+) for scoped threads that can borrow the parent's data.

Run this.
successMessage: Scoped threads can borrow data from the enclosing scope — safe because scope() guarantees threads end before the scope exits.

title: Send and Sync
instruction:
`Send` means a type can be sent to another thread. `Sync` means it can be shared.

Run this to see the rules in practice.
successMessage: Send and Sync are marker traits. Arc<Mutex<T>> is both. The compiler automatically verifies thread safety via these traits.
